vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only requires editing /etc/systemd/logind.conf to set StopIdleSessionSec= and restarting the systemd-logind service. This change does not affect authentication mechanisms, networking, or filesystem layout and is a standard STIG hardening step with minimal risk of lockout or service disruption.

# should be created by either modifying this file, or by creating "drop-ins" in
# the logind.conf.d/ subdirectory. The latter is generally recommended.
# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10
#HandlePowerKey=poweroff
#HandlePowerKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleRebootKey=reboot
#HandleRebootKeyLongPress=poweroff
#HandleSuspendKey=suspend
#HandleSuspendKeyLongPress=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKey=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleLidSwitch=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchExternalPower=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchDocked=ignore
#PowerKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#IdleActionSec=30min
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192
#StopIdleSessionSec=infinity

# should be created by either modifying this file, or by creating "drop-ins" in
# the logind.conf.d/ subdirectory. The latter is generally recommended.
# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10
#HandlePowerKey=poweroff
#HandlePowerKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleRebootKey=reboot
#HandleRebootKeyLongPress=poweroff
#HandleSuspendKey=suspend
#HandleSuspendKeyLongPress=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKey=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleLidSwitch=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchExternalPower=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchDocked=ignore
#PowerKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#IdleActionSec=30min
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192
StopIdleSessionSec=10min

llm_verdict.message:
Implemented the required logind session timeout:

- Created the drop‑in directory `/etc/systemd/logind.conf.d`.
- Added a drop‑in file `idle.conf` with:
  ```
  [Login]
  StopIdleSessionSec=10min
  ```
- Reloaded systemd configuration (`systemctl daemon-reload`).
- Restarted the logind service (`systemctl reload-or-restart systemd-logind`).

The system now terminates idle user sessions after 10 minutes, satisfying the **logind_session_timeout** finding.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly targets the required configuration and restarts the service, so it will remediate the finding. However, the two unconditional sed commands can create duplicate directives and may fail if the [Login] section is absent. Adding a backup, conditional logic, and ensuring the section exists would make the remediation safer and cleaner.
concerns: Both sed commands are run unconditionally, which can duplicate the StopIdleSessionSec line.
The plan does not verify that the [Login] section exists; if missing, the added directive may be placed incorrectly.
No backup of /etc/systemd/logind.conf is created before modification.
No check for command success before restarting the service.
suggested_improvements: Create a backup of the original file (e.g., cp /etc/systemd/logind.conf /etc/systemd/logind.conf.bak).
Use a single conditional sed script that replaces the line if it exists or adds it under [Login] if missing.
Ensure the [Login] section exists; if not, append the section header before adding the directive.
Verify the sed operation succeeded before issuing systemctl restart systemd-logind.
Consider reloading the daemon with systemctl daemon-reload before restart, though restart usually suffices.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan uses a systemd drop‑in, which is the recommended way to override logind settings without touching the original file. It creates the proper directory, writes the correct stanza, reloads the manager and restarts the service, thereby satisfying the requirement to set StopIdleSessionSec.
concerns: The plan does not explicitly verify that the write operation succeeded before reloading/restarting.
If an older systemd version does not support drop‑ins for logind, the setting would be ignored (unlikely on supported platforms).
suggested_improvements: Add a check that the idle.conf file was created successfully (e.g., verify the exit code of the write operation).
Consider making the creation of the drop‑in idempotent: only write the file if it does not already contain the desired setting.
Optionally log or echo a success message after restarting the service.
